sections.paragraph:
[unused in Kal] to set, to put, to place, i.q. נָצַב, from which Niphal, Hiphil, and Hophal, and also many derivative nouns are formed.

Hithpael-

(1) to set oneself, to take a stand (fich hinftellen, hintreten), Exo 2:4 19:17 34:5 Num 11:16 22:22 1Sa 17:16, “and took his stand (for the fight) forty days.” Job 33:5, followed by לִפְנֵי Exo 8:16 and עַל of pers. הִתְיַצֵּב עַל יְהֹוָה used of the angels as presenting themselves before God, Gr. παραστῆναι, Luk 1:19. Job 1:6 Zec 6:5, comp. Pro 22:29. The same phrase in a hostile sense, to stand up against God, Psa 2:2.

(2) to stand (ftehn, daftehn), followed by לִפְנֵי before any one, i.e. to minister to him, Pro 22:29 to stand firm before any one (beftehn vor jem.), whether a victor before an enemy, followed by לִפְנֵי Deu 9:2 Job 41:2 בִּפְנֵי Deu 7:24 11:25 Jos 1:5 עִם 2Ch 20:6 or a just person before a judge, followed by לְנֶגֶד בְּעֵינֵי Psa 5:6. Absol. 2Sa 21:5.

(3) to stand up for, to stand by any one (beiftehn), followed by לְ of pers. Psa 94:16.- Exo 2:4, תֵּתַצַּב ἀνομαλῶς, for תִּתְיַצַּב, see Lehrg. p. 386.
